mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or anchored near zero while CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, equity discount rates stay compressed and valuation multiples expand, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining and CPIAUCSL inflation is below its 12-month average, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income growth and lower earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average 20-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand strength, which should lift earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition on RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-strong instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling FEDFUNDS signals improving real activity without an offsetting tightening shock, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower than 1 month ago
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal fails to deliver positive excess returns versus the baseline in the triggered sample, or if its sign flips negative in any 3-year rolling window with at least 30 triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A high or rising FEDFUNDS rate increases the discount rate on equities and tightens financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month once policy is clearly restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of observations above the threshold, or if the average forward return above 4.5% is not lower than when FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS together with a falling UNRATE indicates broad labor-market improvement, which supports consumer spending and should lift S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not generate positive 60-day returns more often than not, or if the signal is dominated by either series alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and HOUST is rising, the economy is likely entering a synchronized demand rebound that should raise expected corporate revenues and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is above its prior quarter and HOUST is above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint expansion condition fails to outperform the unconditional distribution of 60-day returns, or if positive outcomes occur in less than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening rebound, rising INDPRO accompanied by improving PAYEMS indicates production and hiring are healing together, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal is not positive in at least 55% of episodes or if its walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 for any 2-year period with sufficient samples.
